scope: Network protocol hardening pass for docs/plans/2026-06-05-networking-2.md

hardening_lanes.guard: drop if lc < 0 or lc > entityStateFields count
hardening_lanes.retail_observation: Retail reads an unsigned byte lc and walks the 0x3a-entry entityStateFields table; no committed HLIL bounds guard was observed before the walk.
hardening_lanes.source_resolution: Source now drops with ERR_DROP before indexing entityStateFields[lc] or walking beyond the recovered retail table.

hardening_lanes.guard: drop if lc < 0 or lc > playerStateFields count
hardening_lanes.retail_observation: Retail reads an unsigned byte lc and walks the 0x3a-entry playerStateFields table; no committed HLIL bounds guard was observed before the walk.
hardening_lanes.source_resolution: Source now drops with ERR_DROP before indexing playerStateFields[lc] or walking beyond the recovered retail table.

hardening_lanes.guard: reject duplicate/out-of-order packets, non-contiguous fragments, negative lengths, read-past-packet lengths, fragment buffer overflow, and terminal reassembly larger than msg->maxsize
hardening_lanes.retail_observation: Retail Netchan_Process strips FRAGMENT_BIT, reads optional qport, validates fragment ordering and lengths, returns false for nonterminal fragments, and rebuilds the accepted terminal fragment into msg->data + 4.

hardening_lanes.guard: ignore already-stored server commands and store new commands in seq & (MAX_RELIABLE_COMMANDS - 1)
hardening_lanes.source_resolution: The parser keeps monotonically increasing clc.serverCommandSequence and only uses the masked index after accepting a newer sequence.

hardening_lanes.guard: ignore already-executed reliable client commands and drop clients that skip sequence numbers
clamp or reject acknowledgements older than reliableSequence - MAX_RELIABLE_COMMANDS before ring-indexed command emission
hardening_lanes.server_action: cl->reliableAcknowledge is set to cl->reliableSequence and parsing returns

hardening_lanes.guard: select command strings through reliableAcknowledge & (MAX_RELIABLE_COMMANDS - 1), reset the string cursor at NUL, and sanitize percent/high-bit bytes to '.' before key folding
reject invalid old snapshots, mismatched old message numbers, and parse-entity ages greater than MAX_PARSE_ENTITIES - 128
diagnostics: Delta from invalid frame (not supposed to happen!).

hardening_lanes.guard: fall back to an uncompressed snapshot when deltaMessage is unavailable, too old for PACKET_BACKUP - 3, or its snapshot entities have rolled out of the ring
ignore packets with cmdCount < 1 or cmdCount > MAX_PACKET_USERCMDS before reading usercmd deltas
client drops illegible server messages and read-past-end server messages; server warns on unexpected client command bytes after optional clientCommand records
diagnostics: CL_ParseServerMessage: read past end of server message
WARNING: bad command byte for client %i

negative_vectors.expected: return qfalse and preserve the accumulated fragment buffer
return qfalse before copying fragment bytes
clamp or return before ring-indexed emission can amplify commands
message is consumed but snapshot is not committed

retail_anchors.observed: Retail reads an 8-bit lc and uses the 0x3a-entry entityStateFields table.
Retail reads an 8-bit lc and uses the 0x3a-entry playerStateFields table.
Retail rejects out-of-order, non-contiguous, and illegal fragments and accepts only terminal reassembly.
Retail parses server reliable acknowledgements, opcodes, and bad-message drop paths.
Retail rejects invalid, mismatched, and parse-entity-stale delta frames.
Retail stores server commands through the reliable command ring.
Retail bounds client usercmd counts before reading keyed usercmd deltas.
Retail consumes the three client-message longs plus sideband byte, validates stale acknowledgements, and dispatches move opcodes.
Retail falls back to uncompressed snapshots when delta sources are too old.

remaining_gaps: Invalid lc handling is source-hardened with ERR_DROP guards because committed retail HLIL does not show a safe pre-walk bounds guard.
Byte-for-byte malicious-packet behavior still needs external capture or controlled retail probing before claiming exact retail crash/drop equivalence.
The retail sideband producers behind CL_RetailClientMessageFlags are now source-modeled; byte-for-byte sideband validation remains a packet-capture follow-up.
